recipes.title: Crispy Pan-Fried Onigirazu
ingredients: 3 cups cooked short-grain white rice, seasoned with salt
recipes.body:
## Notes

Fair warning: regular tuna sandwiches are never going to hit the same after this.

## Instructions

1. Mix tuna with mayo, Sriracha, salt, and pepper. Set aside.
2. Fry the eggs and prep all fillings before building.
3. Dip 2 rice paper sheets in warm water, then overlap them on a cutting board. Layer 2 nori sheets on top.
4. Cut 2 slits to create 4 sections. Place rice in one section, tuna across from it, egg in the third, and avocado and cucumber in the fourth.
5. Fold rice over avocado, fold that stack onto the tuna, fold tuna over the egg, fold egg over to the rice, then fold rice over cucumber.
6. Wrap the whole square in 2 more wet rice paper sheets, press to seal, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
7. Pan fry in neutral oil on medium-high heat, searing all sides until golden and crispy.
8. Slice in half.

## Notes

NA

## Instructions

1. Peel the potatoes and boil in salted water until fork-tender. Let cool, then slice.
2. Thinly slice the onion and cook in the butter until soft, reserving the melted butter.
3. Whisk together cream, milk, and eggs, seasoning with salt and pepper.
4. Layer the sliced potatoes, smoked salmon, cooked onions, and fresh dill in a baking dish, pouring a bit of the cream mixture between each layer. Finish with a final layer of the cream mixture on top.
5. Bake at 175°C for about 30 minutes, until the top is golden and bubbly.
6. Drizzle with the reserved melted butter and garnish with a sprinkle of fresh dill.
I love serving it with a simple green salad tossed in lemon juice. Enjoy!
